title: KB Arena: a benchmark for retrieval architectures on your own documentation
description: KB Arena benchmarks nine retrieval architectures (naive vector, contextual vector, QnA pairs, knowledge graph, hybrid via reciprocal rank fusion, RAPTOR, PageIndex, BM25, rerank-vector) head-to-head on user-provided documentation. It auto-generates benchmark questions across five difficulty tiers, computes a layered IR metric stack (Recall@k, Precision@k, Hit@k, MRR, NDCG@k, MAP, R-Precision, bpref) with bootstrap 95% confidence intervals, supports graded NDCG with exponential gain, includes a Rank-Biased Overlap implementation for strategy-vs-strategy comparison, and ships an automated hyperparameter search (kb-arena optimize) that reports Wilcoxon paired significance tests, win-rate, and a Pareto frontier over the (quality, latency) plane.
